quoteLast: . I'd just come up to take a look-see, and saw him just about two miles away, on the surface, and moving right along. So I went under to get into a good position, came up again, and let him have one. Well, he saw it just as it was almost on him, swung her round, and dived like a ton of lead."
title:
World's War Events $v Volume 3
Beginning with the departure of the first American destroyers for service abroad in April, 1917, and closing with the treaties of peace in 1919.
author: Reynolds, Francis J. (Francis Joseph), 1867-1937 [Editor]; Churchill, Allen L. (Allen Leon), 1873-1940 [Editor]

quoteFirst: The Duc de Reichstadt had, indeed, died on 22 July, at
quoteLast: in the morning, the anniversary day on which letters-patent from the emperor had appointed him Duc de Reichstadt, and on which he had learnt of the death of his father the Emperor Napoleon.
title: My Memoirs, Vol. VI, 1832 to 1833
author: Dumas, Alexandre, 1802-1870; Waller, E. M. (Emily Mary) [Translator]

quoteFirst: He soon made a sound of farewell; the priest who was present showed him heaven opening before him and, at
quoteLast: , without a convulsion or a struggle, without even any pain, he gave up his last sigh.
title: My Memoirs, Vol. VI, 1832 to 1833
author: Dumas, Alexandre, 1802-1870; Waller, E. M. (Emily Mary) [Translator]
